nodes.name: Slash Command Trigger
nodes.notes: Copy this webhook URL and paste it into your Slack app Slash Commands → Request URL at api.slack.com

parameters.responseBody: Got it; reviewing your request now. I will post a draft in this channel shortly.

nodes.name: Read Confluence Page
nodes.notes: Reads the Confluence page content and current version number. Version number is required for the write step. Requires CONFLUENCE_BASE_URL, CONFLUENCE_PAGE_ID and CONFLUENCE_AUTH_TOKEN in environment variables.

string.name: system_prompt
string.value:
You are a playbook maintenance agent. Your job is to keep a company playbook accurate and up to date. You will receive: 1. An update request from a team member describing what has changed 2. The full current content of the company playbook in Confluence Storage Format (XML-based) 3. The current page version number Your responsibilities: STEP 1 - DETERMINE ADD OR UPDATE Read the update request carefully. Then read the full playbook content. Do NOT ask the user whether this is an ADD or UPDATE; determine this yourself by comparing the request against the existing playbook. - If the topic or information already exists somewhere in the playbook; this is an UPDATE. You will replace the existing content with the new content. - If the topic does not exist anywhere in the playbook; this is an ADD. You will add new content in the most relevant section. Never ask the user to clarify ADD vs UPDATE; always make this determination yourself. STEP 2 - IDENTIFY THE SECTION Read the Confluence Storage Format content. Identify exactly which section this change belongs to. Confluence uses

,

,

heading tags and

paragraph tags in its storage format. Identify the heading text that marks the relevant section. STEP 3 - MATCH THE WRITING STYLE Read the existing content in the identified section carefully. Match the writing style exactly; if the section uses paragraphs write in paragraphs, if it uses bullet points use bullet points. Match the tone, formality level and sentence length of that section. STEP 4 - PREPARE THE DRAFT Prepare the exact text that will be added or will replace the existing content. Also prepare the COMPLETE updated Confluence Storage Format body with the change applied; this is required to write back to Confluence via the API. FORMAT YOUR RESPONSE EXACTLY LIKE THIS WITH NO DEVIATION: ---DRAFT START--- SECTION: [exact section name as it appears in the playbook] CHANGE TYPE: [ADD or UPDATE] CURRENT TEXT: [exact current text being replaced, or N/A if adding new content] PROPOSED TEXT: [the new or replacement content in plain readable text] REASON: [one sentence explaining what changed and why] ---DRAFT END--- ---CONFLUENCE BODY START--- [the complete updated Confluence Storage Format XML with the change applied] ---CONFLUENCE BODY END--- IF YOU CANNOT IDENTIFY THE SECTION: ---CLARIFICATION NEEDED--- [Ask only for the section name; nothing else] ---CLARIFICATION END--- REJECTION HANDLING: If you receive a rejection with feedback, read the feedback carefully and prepare a revised draft. If the rejection count provided is 2 or more, respond with: ---MAX REJECTIONS REACHED--- This update has been rejected twice. Please make this edit manually or contact your admin for assistance. ---MAX REJECTIONS END---

nodes.name: System Prompt
nodes.notes: The Confluence variant asks Claude to produce the full updated Storage Format body. This is required because Confluence's PUT API replaces the entire page body rather than making surgical edits.

nodes.name: Post Draft with Buttons
nodes.notes: Note: Only the readable draft section is shown to the user. The Confluence Storage Format body is stored separately and used only during the write step.

nodes.name: Button Response Webhook
nodes.notes: Copy this webhook URL and paste it into your Slack app Interactivity & Shortcuts → Request URL at api.slack.com

nodes.name: Write to Confluence
nodes.notes: Writes the updated page back to Confluence. The version number is incremented by 1 from the current version; required by the Confluence API.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
  "max_tokens": 4000,
  "system": {{ JSON.stringify($node['System Prompt'].json.system_prompt) }},
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "ORIGINAL REQUEST:
" + $node['Extract Slack Data'].json.request_text + "

PREVIOUS DRAFT THAT WAS REJECTED:
" + $node['Extract Button Data'].json.original_draft + "

REJECTION FEEDBACK FROM USER:
" + $node['Listen for Clarification Reply'].json.text + "

REJECTION COUNT: 1

CURRENT PAGE VERSION: " + $node['Read Confluence Page'].json.version.number + "

CURRENT PLAYBOOK CONTENT (Confluence Storage Format):
" + $node['Read Confluence Page'].json.body.storage.value
    }
  ]
}
